km.France: ប្រទេសបារាំង

zh.lifeSummary: Wautier V de Fontaine l'évèque和N.N. de Sottenghien至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Wautier V de Fontaine l'évèque和N.N. de Sottenghien至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Fontaine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Normand, Pierre, Emile, Fernand, Lucien, Marcel, Adelard, Henri, Jacques, Laurent.

French and Walloon: topographic name for someone living near a spring or well, from Old French fontaine, from Late Latin fontana, a derivative of Latin fons (see Font ); or a habitational name from Fontaine, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Fountaine .
Shortened form of French   Lafontaine  , a variant of 1 above.

variants.name: Fontenot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Ulysse, Andrus, Alcide, Andre, Edouard, Lucien, Marise, Monique, Remy.

French: variant of Fonteneau (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a topographic name from a diminutive of Old French fontaine ‘spring, well’ (see Fontaine ), or a habitational name from any of several places mainly in the westcentral part of France so named. The surname Fontenot is very rare in France.
History: Jean-Louis Fonteneau/Fontenot from Montierneuf in Vienne, France, married Louise-Angélique Henry in Mobile, AL, in 1726.

variants.name: Fontenelle
meanings: French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French fontaine ‘spring, well’ (see Fontaine ), or a habitational name from (La) Fontenelle, the name of several places in various parts of France, of the same origin.

variants.name: Fontenette
meanings: French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French fontaine ‘spring, well’ (see Fontaine ), or a habitational name from Fontenette, the name of several places mainly in the central part of France, of the same origin.

variants.name: Fountain
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from one or more of the places in France named Fontaine, Fonteyne, or Lafontaine (see   Fontaine  ).

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a spring or well, from Middle English fontayne (Old French fontaine) ‘spring, fountain’, sometimes with a plural -s, and synonymous with Well , of which Fountain(es) may sometimes have been a translation.

variants.name: Font
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Luis, Miguel, Rafael, Eduardo, Raul, Eugenio, Pedro, Roberto, Aida.
Catalan and southern French: topographic name for someone living near a spring or well, Catalan and Occitan font ‘spring, well’ (from Latin fons, genitive fontis).
English: variant of   Fant  .

variants.name: Fontan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Angel, Benigno, Carlos, Jose, Juana, Manuel, Pedro, Adolfo, Aida, Alfredo, Candido, Emilia.
Spanish and Galician (Fontán): habitational name from any of the many places called Fontán, from fontán ‘spring’.
French: variant of   Fontaine  .

nameMeanings.name: l'évèque
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Henri, Andre, Antoine, Auguste, Edouard, Emile, Germaine, Lucien, Marie-France, Yves.
French (Lévêque): variant of Lévesque (see Levesque ).

variants.name: Levesque
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Normand, Armand, Lucien, Marcel, Rosaire, Emile, Gilles, Pierre, Jacques, Adrien, Raoul.

French: from Old French évesque ‘bishop’ (from Latin episcopus; see Bishop ), with fused definite article l', hence an occupational name for a member of a bishop's household, or an ironic nickname for a solemn person. Compare Laveck , Leveck , and Leveque .

nameMeanings.name: Wautier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French (mainly Normandy): from the personal name Vautier, a regional variant of Gauthier .
History: This surname was first brought to North America by Dominique Vautier born 1766 in Meuse, France, died 1828 in PA.

variants.name: Votaw
meanings: Altered form of French Vauteau: possibly a derivative of the personal name Vautier . The surname Vauteau has apparently died out in France. Compare Voter .

History: The Votaws trace their origin to Isaac Vauteau from France, who in the 1680s immigrated to England and later settled in Ireland. During the 18th century some of his children and grandchildren emigrated from Ireland to the US.

variants.name: Vautrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Franck, Lydie, Normand.
French (mainly Lorraine): from a pet form of the personal name   Vautier   ‘Walter’. Compare   Votra   and   Wotring  .

History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered form Wotring) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. The Huguenot ancestor was Abraham Vautrin, Jr (also Wautrin) from Lorraine, France, whose descendants bear the surnames Wotring , Watring, and Woodring. — The progenitor of the American bearers of the surname Vautrin was Charles Vautrin, a soldier from France, who was in QC by 1760, when he married Marie-Catherine Boire. His descendants also bear the surnames such as Votra .

variants.name: Gauthier
meanings: Germanic. See Walter .
